easy.question: What is the purpose and scope of the EV Charging Policy?
easy.answer: To support tenants in general needs homes to install EV charging where feasible, managing safety/compliance risks as demand grows. It applies to tenant requests in general needs homes. :contentReference[oaicite:46]{index=46}

easy.question: Who pays for installing an EV charger in an existing general needs home?
easy.answer: The tenant funds purchase, installation and maintenance, using an approved installer and obtaining Midland Heart approval first. :contentReference[oaicite:47]{index=47}

easy.question: Does Midland Heart install EV chargers in existing general needs homes?
easy.answer: No—Midland Heart is not responsible for installing EV chargers where Building Regulations 2021 Part S does not apply. :contentReference[oaicite:48]{index=48}

easy.question: What installer requirements apply?
easy.answer: Tenants must use an appropriately approved installer (e.g., from the GOV.UK approved list) and provide installation documentation on completion. :contentReference[oaicite:49]{index=49}

easy.question: Who maintains a tenant-installed charger during the tenancy?
easy.answer: The tenant, with evidence of maintenance/warranty as required at set intervals. :contentReference[oaicite:50]{index=50}

easy.question: What happens to a charger at tenancy end?
easy.answer: The tenant may remove it safely (isolating wiring). If left, Midland Heart inspects/tests; if compliant, it can remain for the incoming tenant. :contentReference[oaicite:51]{index=51}

easy.question: If a charger remains for the new tenant, who is responsible?
easy.answer: Depending on condition/compliance and access to keys/locking devices, ownership may transition to Midland Heart; responsibility for maintenance/testing then follows policy rules. :contentReference[oaicite:52]{index=52}

easy.question: Is charger testing included in EICR cycles?
easy.answer: Yes—device testing is factored into the five-yearly EICR cycle. :contentReference[oaicite:53]{index=53}

medium.question: Outline the approval process a tenant follows to install a new EV charger.
medium.answer: Submit a request; Midland Heart surveys for suitability; tenant uses an approved installer; provides completion documentation; maintenance responsibilities and any conditions are confirmed (see flowchart on page 4). :contentReference[oaicite:54]{index=54}

medium.question: A void property contains a charger. What decides whether it stays?
medium.answer: Electrical safety test/inspection and review of maintenance records; if compliant, it can stay; otherwise it’s removed. :contentReference[oaicite:55]{index=55}

medium.question: A new tenant doesn’t want to use the existing charger. What happens?
medium.answer: It’s locked off and remains under Midland Heart’s management until the tenancy ends, unless removed per policy. :contentReference[oaicite:56]{index=56}

medium.question: Name the internal teams/roles involved in EV requests and oversight.
medium.answer: EV Team (senior contracts manager, contract managers), electrical survey team (qualified persons), with ESG Committee oversight of policy delivery/updates. :contentReference[oaicite:57]{index=57}

medium.question: What data-protection stance is stated?
medium.answer: Policies and procedures are developed in line with Midland Heart’s Data Protection policy to meet legal obligations. :contentReference[oaicite:58]{index=58}

medium.question: Which best-practice and accessibility standards are referenced?
medium.answer: IET Code of Practice for EV Charging Installations (4th ed.), PAS 1899:2022 for accessible charging, and IEC 62955 DC fault detection. :contentReference[oaicite:59]{index=59}

medium.question: If the charger fails Midland Heart’s requirements at re-let, what is the outcome?
medium.answer: The charger is removed if it does not meet requirements (condition, compliance, documentation, access devices). :contentReference[oaicite:60]{index=60}

medium.question: Where can tenants find approved installers?
medium.answer: On the GOV.UK ‘Find an EV chargepoint installer’ service, referenced in the policy. :contentReference[oaicite:61]{index=61}

hard.question: Map responsibilities when a tenant leaves a charger in situ with full documentation and the incoming tenant opts in to use it.
hard.answer: Midland Heart inspects/tests for electrical safety; if compliant and with full access (keys/locking), ownership may transition to Midland Heart; the new tenant declares use and associated maintenance costs transfer under their contract; Midland Heart then undertakes periodic testing per EICR cycles. :contentReference[oaicite:62]{index=62}

hard.question: A survey finds DC leakage protection absent. What policy/standards guide remediation?
hard.answer: Align the installation with IEC 62955 DC fault detection and the IET Code of Practice; if requirements cannot be met, the charger should be removed or locked off until compliant. :contentReference[oaicite:63]{index=63}

hard.question: Distinguish responsibilities between existing general needs homes and situations where Part S of Building Regulations applies.
hard.answer: In existing general needs homes (Part S not applicable), tenants fund/install via approved installers with MH approval. Where Part S applies (e.g., certain new builds), MH responsibility is dictated by Building Regulations 2021 Part S. :contentReference[oaicite:64]{index=64}

hard.question: Installer identifies extra works (e.g., consumer-unit upgrade). How is this handled?
hard.answer: The tenant remains responsible for installation costs; additional works identified must be addressed to meet MH requirements and standards before approval/completion. :contentReference[oaicite:65]{index=65}

hard.question: A request arises from a retirement/supported scheme. What does scope say?
hard.answer: The policy applies to general needs homes only; other schemes fall outside this policy and would follow separate guidance/approvals. :contentReference[oaicite:66]{index=66}

hard.question: Set out records and governance to evidence policy delivery at scale.
hard.answer: Maintain survey/approval records, installation documentation, safety/maintenance logs, EICR test results, and report to the ESG Committee on delivery/updates; train colleagues to verify install/isolation/removal. :contentReference[oaicite:67]{index=67}

hard.question: How are chargers treated at re-let when documentation is incomplete?
hard.answer: Without suitable documentation/compliance, MH removes the charger rather than transferring it to a new tenant. :contentReference[oaicite:68]{index=68}
